content.full_text:
Re
Farn PolrrrcAI- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
December 13,2005
Alan J. Smith
City of Watsonville
P.O. Box 5000
Watsonville, CA 95077 -5000
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. ,4'-05-205
Dear Mr. Smith:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of two council members, Oscar
Rios and Antonio Rivas, of the City of Watsonville for advice regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act").1 This letter should not be
construed as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. In addition,
this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission
("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71; Govt. Code section 83114.)
QUESTTON
Can two members of the city council each solicit and receive contributions into
their campaign accounts to reimburse them for their expenses incurred for travel to
Pinghu City in the Republic of China for the purpose of exploring the possibility of
establishing a sister city relationship between Pinghu City and the City of Watsonville?2
CONCLUSION
Yes. The city council members may solicit and use campaign funds to reimburse
them for such expenses if the expenditures are directly related to a legislative or
governmental purpose and they follow the procedures set forth in the Act as specified in
section 8951 1.5.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.
2 Your letter presented tlree possii'le scenarios for payrnent/reimbursement of these travel
expenses. In our recent telephone conversation, you requested that we limit our analysis to the most
practicable option.
n6B


File No. A-05-2,05
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are writing on behalf of two council members of the City of Watsonville.
Both council members have open campaignacoounts. The council members are leaving
on a trip to Pinghu CitS Republic of China. The purpose of the trip is to explore the
possibility of establishing a sister city relationship between the City of WatJonville and
Pinghu city. The city of watsonville has other jister city relation.hipr.
No money has yet been formally solicited or received by either council member.
Both intend to make the trip and then solicit contributions fromthe community to
reimburse them for the expense of the trip. No city funds are being used for the trip.
ANALYSIS
The Political Reform Act's provisions conceming the permissible uses of
campaignfunds are found in sections 89510-89522, copGs enclosed. Under these
provisions, camBaign funds are regarded as totally separate and distinct from a candidate
or officeholder's personal funds. (KaufrnanAdvice Let{er,No. 4_04_055.) The Act,s
campaign funds provisions are designed to ensure that campaign funds areused for
electioneering and officeholder pu{poses, and are not egpend"d for the candidate or
offi ceholder's personal purposes. (Kaufman, supra.)
The Act provides that all contributions deposited into a campaign account are
'held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the candidate or for expenses
associated with holding office." (Section 89510.) Generally speaking, an expenditure
related to seeking or holding office is permissible under the Act if it G ..reasonably
related" to a political,legislative or goveflrmental purpose. (Sectio n g9512.) Certain
uses of campaign funds, including travel, that are Considered to confer a..sutstantial
personal benefit"3 on the candidate must meet a higher standard and be .directly related
to a political, legislative, or governmentar purpose.;, (Section gg5l2.)
With respect to the use of campaign funds for travel, section 89513 states:
- 
"(a) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay or reimburse
the candidate, the elected officer, or any individual tr individuals
with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held
by a committee, or employees or staff of the commitiee or the
elected officer's governmental agency for travel expenses and
necessary accommodations except when these expenditures are
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
"S'tlstantial personal benefit is defined as "an expenditure of campaign funds which results in a
direct personal benefit ofmore than two hundred dollars ($ioo; to a candidite, Jlected officer, or any
individual or individual with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a committee.,,
(Section 8951 1(b).)


File No. A-05-205
Page No. 3
(1) For the purposes of this section, payments or
reimbursements for travel and necessary accommodations shall be
considered as directly related to a politital, legislative, or
govefllmental purpose if the payments would meet standards
similar to the standards of the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to
sections 162 and274 of the Intemal Revenue code for deductions
of travel expenses under the federal income tax law.,, (Emphasis
added.)
Intemal Revenue Code section 162(a)Q) sets forth general rules which permit the
deduction of all ordintry and necessary expenses while traveling away from home in the
pursuit of a trade or business.* Under the Act, these rules have been applied to permit the
use of campaign funds for only the portions of a trip when political, legislative, or
govefirmental "business" is conducted. To the extent that any day is not a business day
(such as days on which there are no meetings or formal activities), campaign funds may
not be used. Moreover, personal activities or expenses not related to a gov-ernmental
pu{pose may not be paid for with campaign funds, even if those activities occur on a
business day. (Hos terman Advice Letter, No. 4-05-044.)
Finally, your question involves whether each council member may be reimbursed
from his or her campaign account after he or she pays for the expenses incurred from
personal funds. Government code section 8951 1.5, subdivision (b) allows an incumbent
elected officer to be reimbursed for expenditures of his or her personal funds from a
campaign bank account if all of the following conditions are met:
"(1) The expenditures are not campaign expenses.
(2) The incumbent elected officer, prior to reimbursement,
provides the keasurer of the committee with a dated receipt and
written description of each expenditure.
(3) Reimbursement is paid within 90 days of the
expenditure, in the case of a cash expenditure, orwithin 90 days of
the end of the billing period in which it was included, in the case of
an expenditure charges to a credit card or charge account.,, 
5
a Inpertinentpart, Internal Revenue Code section 162(a) states that:
"(a) In general. There shall be allowed as a deduction all the ordinary and necessary expenses paid or
incurred during the taxable year in carrytngon any trade or business, including -
(1) a reasonable allowance for salaries or other compensation for personal iervices actually rendered;
Q) traveling expenses (including amountsfor meals and lodgingither than amounts whrih are lavish or
extrqvagant under the circumstance) while away from home in the pursuit of a trade or business. . . .,,
(Emphasis added.)
' Subdivision (e) of section 895 1 I .5 notes tlnt the section shall not be construed to authorize
expenditures other than those associated with holding office, i.e., as in this case, an expenditure directly
related to a legislative or goverrmental purposes.


File No. A-05-205
Page No. 4
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By:
t
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
WJL:rd
